title: crossonephelis unijugatus
intro: Crossonephelis unijugatus, also known as the "one-joined kidney vine," is a tropical plant species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique kidney-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Crossonephelis unijugatus include reducing fever, soothing wounds and skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
